lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Getting Things Done -- Can/Could for requests (A1)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Estephano Akihito Ishii | Aula 12 -- Getting Things Done

listenings.text: Good morning, team. Before the meeting, I have three small requests. First, can you open the windows, please? The room is very hot today. Then, could you bring the coffee to the big table? And one more thing: please send me the report by email before lunch. Thank you very much for your help. Have a great day.

I work in a big office, and I often need help. When I need a document, I say: Could you send me the file, please? When my hands are full, I ask a friend: Can you open the door for me? People here are very kind, and they always say yes. To be polite, I always say please and thank you. Small words make a big difference.

extra_audio.text: Estephano needs help at work. First, he asks: Can you help me, please? Then he says: Could you send me the file? After that, he asks: Could you open the window? Next, he says: Can I borrow your pen? Finally, he says: Thank you very much.
